title: STIG file-integrity hash mismatch (FISMA / FedRAMP)

compliance.assurance_rationale: FISMA §3554(b)(5) — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that FISMA §3554(b)(5) (Security controls and monitoring) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.19.3: STIG file-integrity hash mismatch (FISMA / FedRAMP).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.19.3' running on CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Tenable/STIG scans, POA&M exports, AAD/O365 sign-in, inventory, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

index=os sourcetype=stash:file_integrity earliest=-7d
| eval uc_tag="22.19.3"
| lookup stig_baseline.csv path OUTPUT expected_hash

description: Strengthens agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP by detecting drift between deployed file-integrity hashes and the STIG baseline across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for FISMA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes os and sourcetypes stash:file_integrity. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=os`.
visualization: Time chart, Table, Single value, Heat map. Key fields: `host`, `path`, `uc_tag`.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services

knownFalsePositives: Continuous monitoring lapses during reporting cycles, control owner transitions, or platform migrations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Tenable/STIG scans, POA&M exports, AAD/O365 sign-in, inventory.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=os sourcetype=stash:file_integrity earliest=-7d
| eval uc_tag="22.19.3"
| lookup stig_baseline.csv path OUTPUT expected_hash
| where sha256!=expected_hash
| stats count by host, path
```

Understanding this SPL

**STIG file-integrity hash mismatch (FISMA / FedRAMP)** — Strengthens agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP by detecting drift between deployed file-integrity hashes and the STIG baseline across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds.

Documented **Data sources**: CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Tenable/STIG scans, POA&M exports, AAD/O365 sign-in, inventory. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: os; **sourcetype**: stash:file_integrity. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=os, sourcetype=stash:file_integrity, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where sha256!=expected_hash` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, path** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the FedRAMP ConMon scan results and the agency or provider evidence pack for the same time window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value, Heat map
grandmaExplanation: We strengthen agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP by detecting drift between deployed file-integrity hashes and the STIG baseline across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds. That backs trust in the cloud and federal program posture with data, not just answers on a checklist.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Change data model (Change.All_Changes).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by user, object, result | sort - count
